bio.biography:
Quick Facts

  • Sport: Basketball

  • Specialty: Power forward / small forward

Biographical Information 

Cooper Flagg is an American basketball player who has made an impact in both high school and college basketball, earning attention from scouts and analysts alike. His ability to contribute across multiple facets of the game—scoring, rebounding and playmaking—has set him apart as a future star. 

Flagg comes from a family of basketball players; his mother played college basketball at the University of Maine and his father played NJCAA at Eastern Maine Community College. He attended Nokomis Regional High School, where he was the first freshman to earn the Maine Gatorade Player of the Year award, and later transferred to Montverde Academy. He is currently a member of Duke’s basketball team (the Duke Blue Devils). 

Off the court, Flagg and his family show their support for the Ronald McDonald House Charities in Portland, Maine; five dollars from every “In Flagg We Trust” tee shirt sold are donated to the charity. 

Olympic Highlights

Flagg was a member of the 2024 USA Select Team who trained in Las Vegas alongside the U.S. Men's National Team as they prepared for the Paris 2024 Olympic Games.

Early Career

Cooper Flagg’s high school career began in Maine and finished at a prestigious program in Florida. He became the first freshman in Maine history to be named Gatorade Player of the Year. In 2022, Flagg participated in the FIBA Under-17 Basketball World Cup in Spain, earning a gold medal and helping the U.S. win their sixth title.

Flagg transferred to Montverde Academy after his freshman year and was named MVP of the 2023 Hoophall Classic after a standout performance.

In 2024, he was selected for the McDonald’s All-American Boys Game. He was named Gatorade National Player of the Year, Mr. Basketball USA and Naismith Prep Player of the Year. Flagg averaged 16.1 points, 7.6 rebounds, 3.9 assists and 2.7 blocks per game. He also led Montverde Academy to a perfect 34-0 record and the program's eighth national title.

In October 2023, Flagg committed to Duke University and finished as the top-rated recruit in the 2024 class. He enrolled in the summer of 2024 to take part in the Blue Devils' summer practices. He was ranked as the third-best prospect in the 2025 recruiting class after his freshman season. Prior to attending Duke, he signed a shoe deal with New Balance and was the first men’s college basketball player to get a sponsorship from Gatorade.

Career Highlights
  • World Championship U-17, 2022 (Team - Men)

bio.education: Valley Christian High School (Spokane Valley, Wash.) ‘23
bio.biography:
Quick Facts
  • Daughter of Clay and Jewel Floch
  • Has a younger sister, Ruby
  • Born with transverse myelitis, which causes her to have weakness from the waist down
  • Adopted from China when she was 10 years old from an orphanage in 2012
  • Discovered Paralympic sport when she was 10 from teammate Phillip Croft who lived in the same orphanage as her in China
  • Also plays wheelchair basketball
  • Hobbies include reading, playing with her dogs, swimming, hanging out with her sister and learning new things.
Career Highlights
  • ParaPan American Games, Lima, 2019 (400m T54 - Women, 100m T54 - Women)

Most Notable

  • Only high school player on Team USA's 2019 World ParaVolley World Super 6 squad that won gold
  • Started training with the U.S. Women's National Sitting Volleyball Team at age 14 in 2017, then earned a spot on the top national team in 2019 while still a sophomore in high school
  • Also plays standing volleyball with Southside Volleyball Club in Salem, Oregon.

Major Competitions

  • 2019: Exhibitions vs Canada at Colorado Crossroads; Exhibitions vs. Russia at USAV Open Nationals; World ParaVolley World Super 6 (Gold)
  • 2018: Exhibitions vs Canada at USAV Open Nationals

Physical Disability: Fibular Hememilia

  • "I was born with only three toes on my right leg. My femur was shorter and my leg was also shorter. I had the option of amputation or keeping it and living my life in a wheelchair - my parents decided to amputate. I had my right foot amputated at nine months old and I got my first prosthetic at 11 months."

Path to the Sitting Volleyball National Team:: "I was introduced to sitting volleyball through a summer camp in Ohio in 2016. Shortly after I started training in the National Team A2 program and about a year later I started training with the national team in November of 2017. I joined the national team in 2019.

Did You Know

  • An athlete involved with Challenged Athletes Foundation
  • Volunteer at the elementary school her mother Linda teaches at and volunteers through the leadership program at her high school (South Salem High School).
  • Works in the nursery at her church.

Happiest Moment in Life: "Seeing my mom beat breast cancer."

Favorites

  • Food: Pasta
  • Book: The Fault in our Stars
  • Movie: Tangled
  • Athlete: Matt Anderson

Most Admired Person: "My mom because she has always been my role model in life and has taught me how to lead a good life and to always be confident with who I am."

Hobbies: Music, being with friends and family

Personal: Parents are Jeff and Linda Flood ... Older sisters are Lindsay and Maggie Flood.

Paralympic Experience
  • 1-time Paralympian; 1-time Paralympic medalist (1 gold)
    • Paralympic Games Tokyo 2020, gold (Team - Women)

Quick Facts

  • Sport: Surfing

  • Specialty: Men’s shortboard

Biographical Information

John John Florence is an American professional surfer and one of the most talented pipe surfers in the world. He was born and raised on the North Shore of Oahu, Hawaii, and grew up just steps away from Pipeline, one of the most famous and dangerous waves in the world. By the time he was five years old, Florence was already surfing and showing incredible talent for the sport. 

Florence has a deep connection to nature and is passionate about sustainability and ocean conservation. He enjoys sailing and spending time with his family and friends in Hawaii. Florence is also the founder of Florence Marine X, an eco-conscious clothing brand dedicated to adventure and environmental sustainability.

Olympic Highlights

Florence proudly represented Team USA at the Tokyo 2020 Olympic Games, when surfing made its historic debut. He qualified and competed in the men's shortboard event reaching the round of 16.

He returned to the Olympics to compete in the Paris 2024 Olympic Games. He reached the third round, falling just shy of his previous mark at the Tokyo 2020 Games.

World Championship Highlights

John John Florence achieved his first World Surf League (WSL) championship title in 2016, establishing himself as one of surfing's elite competitors. In 2017, he defended his WSL championship title, becoming a two-time world champion, and the first Hawaii-born surfer to secure back-to-back championship titles since Andy Irons. 

Seven years later in 2024, Florence achieved his third WSL championship title in 2024, making him one of the most decorated U.S. surfers on the world stage, and tying him with Tom Curren for second-most all-time for an American surfer. 

Early Career

At just 13 years old, Florence became the youngest surfer ever to compete in the Vans Triple Crown of Surfing, a prestigious Hawaiian surfing series. He quickly made a name for himself on the Qualifying Series.

By 2011, Florence had qualified for the World Surf League (WSL) Championship Tour, where he began competing against the world’s top surfers. His career breakthrough came in 2016 when he won his first WSL World Title.

Olympic Experience
  • 2-time Olympian
    • Olympic Games Paris 2024, 9th (Shortboard - Men)
    • Olympic Games Tokyo 2020, 9th (Shortboard - Men)
World Championships Experience
  • Most recent: 2021 – 10th
  • Years of participation: 2011, 2012, 2013, 2014, 2015, 2016, 2017, 2018, 2019, 2021
  • Medals: 2 (2 gold)
    • Gold – 2017, 2016

bio.fun_fact: College Alpine Skier for Clarkson University
bio.biography:
Quick Facts
  • College alpine ski racer at Clarkson University (Potsdam, New York)
  • Picked up skeleton heading into his junior year in college after a family trip to Lake Placid
Biographical Information

Austin Florian grew up in Southington, Connecticut, and started to ski at age 2. By age 6, Florian was competing in ski racing competitions and played other sports in high school, including lacrosse and golf. 

Florian went to Clarkson University in Potsdam, New York, where he was a four-year member of Clarkson's ski team. Because of his proximity to Lake Placid, his family stopped in the city that hosted the 1932 and 1980 Winter Olympics while driving him back to school, and he took in all the typical tourist activities, which included "land luge" - a luge sled on rollerblade-style wheels. Showing promise, Florian decided to pursue an open tryout for U.S. sliding sports later that fall and began balancing skeleton training with ski training in his final two years of college. 

By the end of his senior year at Clarkson, Florian was entering North American cup races in skeleton and within a few years, he finished in the top 10 in just his second IBSF World Cup race in January 2019.

Florian competed for the U.S. in multiple world championships, winning gold at the 2025 IBSF World Championships in mixed team skeleton, an event that will debut at the Olympic Winter Games Milano Cortina 2026. 

Florian graduated from Clarkson University with a degree in engineering. His wife, Erica, is a former Division I soccer player. 

World Championships Experience

  • Years of participation: Individual - Men 2019, 2020, 2021, 2023, 2024, 2025; Team Competition - Mixed 2019, 2020, 2023, 2024, 2025
  • Medals: 1 (1 gold)
    • Gold – 2025 (Team Competition - Mixed)

GETTING TO KNOW FLOWERS

I love watching ALL kinds of movies. I like to go to sleep watching Disney movies...they give me good dreams :) My favorite TV shows are Everybody Loves Raymond, the Fresh Prince of Bel-Air, and a new show called The Game. My “top 5” would be Jason, mom, dad, Tash and Selena. My dream job would be a sports broadcaster, but ironically I get nervous talking in front of people...go figure. My perfect day would be to sleep in, play softball and come home and watch movies. If I had a super power it would be instant traveling. I hate to drive and travel and just want to get there as soon as possible. My wedding anniversary is August 23, 2003 so the Olympic Gold Medal game in 2004 was our first anniversary. My husband is Jason Flowers and I have a step daughter Jasmine. I would like to own a dog but am gone too much. My dad did give us a robot dog for Christmas so that will work for now. If I could be any teammate I would be Laura Berg so that I could be the lefty slapper and I could see what it’s like to play pranks all the time and get away with it. I’m an only child of Leroy and Jackie Mims. I am half black and half white. I was born January 9, 1981. I really don’t like talking on the phone.

  • 2012-2013 Four-Man World Cup for pilots Nick Cunningham and Steve Holcomb -BRONZE in Lake Placid (11/12); 5th in Park City (11/12); 11th in Whistler (11/12); 11th in Winterberg (12/12); 12th in La Plagne (12/12); 7th in Koenigssee (1/13); 17th in Igls (1/13); 13th in Sochi (2/13)
  • 2011-2012 Two-Man World Cup for pilot John Napier -11th in La Plagne (12/11); 12th in Koenigssee (1/12); 16th in St. Moritz (1/12)
  • 2011-2012 Four-Man World Cup for pilot John Napier -12th in Igls (12/11); 13th in La Plagne (12/11); 15th in Winterberg (12/11); 16th in Altenberg (1/12); 13th in Koenigssee (1/12); 12th in St. Moritz (1/12)
  • 2011-2012 Two-Man European Cup for pilot John Napier -7th in Igls #1 (11/11)
  • 2011-2012 Four-Man European Cup for pilot John Napier -12th in Igls #1 (11/11); 9th in Igls #2 (11/11)
  • 2009-2010 Four-Man World Cup for pilots Todd Hays and John Napier -6th in Park City (11/09), SILVER in Lake Placid (11/09), 5th in Winterberg (12/09), 10th in Altenberg (12/09), BRONZE in Konigsse (1/10), 11th in St. Moritz (1/10), 13th in Igls (1/10)

  • 2008-2009 Two-Man North American Cup -SILVER in Lake Placid (4/1)

bio.education: Wooster High School (Wooster, Ohio) ‘15 Stark State College of Technology ’18, Graphic Arts
bio.biography:
Quick Facts
  • Son of Brett and Lisa Followay
  • Has three sisters, Madyson, Mikinley and Schae
  • Born with spina bifida
  • Hobbies include sled hockey, wheelchair basketball and graphic arts
  • Began wheelchair racing at eight years old
  • Became the first wheelchair racer to join his high school track team in the Ohio High School Athletic Association
  • Has an asphalt nature trail named in his honor in his hometown of Wooster, Ohio
  • Volunteers at Adaptive Sports Program of Ohio, founded by his mother, Lisa.
Career Highlights
  • ParaPan American Games, Lima, 2019 (100m T53 - Men)

Quick Facts
  • Paraplegic following an automobile accident when 16-years-old
  • Career in table tennis began after the accident at the age of 17, but retired three years later to pursue her dream of playing Paralympic basketball.
  • After retiring from basketball, returned to table tennis in 2006
  • Hobbies include racket sports, basketball, and card games. Favorite television shows are CSI and Criminal Minds.
Career Highlights
  • 2015 Para Pan American Games, team member
Paralympic Experience
  • 3-time Paralympian; 1-time Paralympic medalist (1 bronze)
    • Paralympic Games Rio de Janeiro 2016, 13th (Singles Class 3 - Women)
    • Paralympic Games London 2012, 9th (Singles Class 3 - Women, Team Class 1-3 - Women)
    • Paralympic Games Atlanta 1996, bronze (Team - Women)
World Championships Experience
  • Most recent: 2022 – 9th (Doubles Class 10 - Women, Doubles Class 7 - Mixed), 17th (Singles Class 3 - Women)
  • Years of participation: Doubles Class 10 - Women 2022; Doubles Class 7 - Mixed 2022; Singles Class 3 - Women 2010, 2022; Open Wheelchair - Women 2010; Team - Women 1994
  • Medals: 1 (1 silver)
    • Silver – 1994 (Team - Women)

Quick Facts
  • Graduated from Cal State Northridge in 2005 with a degree in sociology
  • Married to Jeff Conover, who coached Jennifer Kessy and April Ross during their silver medal performance at the 2012 Olympic Games
  • Son Bowen was born in 2018
  • Father Everett Fopma played pro basketball in Europe. Mother is Anita Fopma. Jennifer has two brothers, Brandon and Garret and a sister, Katy
  • Was born in Leiden, Holland, where her father was playing professional basketball; family moved to California when Fopma was one
Career Highlights
  • Career best FIVB World Tour finish was bronze in 2013 with Brooke Sweat
  • Has three career international medals, including gold on the NORCECA Continental Tour from 2013
  • Was Cal State Northridge's first volleyball All-American in school history
  • ---
  • DID YOU KNOW?
  • In 2017, Fopma played exclusively on the AVP Tour, with two third-place finishes, two fifth and two ninth
  • In 2016, Fopma finished ninth on three occasions on the FIVB World Tour; on the domestic AVP Tour, she earned one silver and one bronze in five events
  • On the domestic AVP Tour, Fopma has three first-place finishes, earning two in 2015
  • Between 2014-15, Fopma had top finishes of fourth and fifth on the FIVB World Tour
  • In 2013 with partner Brooke Sweat, top finishes on the FIVB World Tour came in Rome (9th), Gstaad (5th), Long Beach (9th) and Berlin (3rd)
  • Began her pro beach career in 2005
  • Fopma began her college career at Pepperdine (1999-2000) before transferring to Cal State Northridge in 2001
  • Was a first-team all-Big West selection and a third-team All-American, becoming the first Division I All-American in CSUN's history
  • Helped both Pepperdine and CSUN to the NCAA Tournament and was named All-Region with both teams
  • Started playing indoor volleyball in 1995 with Ichiban Long Beach
